source.title: China express delivery volume 2025 and Pinduoduo share estimation

extractions.claim: China processed 216.5 billion express parcels in 2025, up 11.5% YoY from ~194B in 2024
extractions.evidence: China's express delivery sector managed 216.5 billion parcels in 2025, marking an 11.5% rise from the previous year and averaging over 141 parcels per person. The 180B milestone was hit by Nov 30. 2024 total: ~194B parcels (from 174B in 2023 + growth).

verification.verifier_notes: From Global Times and State Post Bureau data. 132B in 2023, 174B in 2024, 216.5B in 2025.

extractions.claim: Pinduoduo's estimated share of China's express parcels is 35-40%, implying 76-87B parcels in 2025
extractions.evidence: Pinduoduo is the largest single source of express parcels in China due to its high-frequency, low-AOV model. Industry estimates (carrier commentary, analyst reports) suggest Pinduoduo generates 35-40% of China's express volume. At 216.5B total: 35% = 75.8B parcels, 40% = 86.6B parcels. Pinduoduo parcels x AOV RMB 50-60 = RMB 3.8-5.2T domestic GMV.

verification.verifier_notes: Pinduoduo's parcel share is not directly disclosed. 35-40% is an analyst consensus range based on: (1) ZTO/YTO carrier commentary about 'low-price platform' volumes, (2) Pinduoduo's dominance in high-frequency categories (FMCG, agriculture). The AOV assumption of RMB 50-60 drives the output significantly.

extractions.claim: Logistics back-calculation supports domestic GMV of RMB 3.8-5.2T, consistent with other methods
extractions.evidence: 76-87B parcels x RMB 50 AOV = RMB 3.8-4.3T. 76-87B parcels x RMB 60 AOV = RMB 4.6-5.2T. Central estimate: ~RMB 4.2-4.8T domestic. This is consistent with Method 1 (RMB 3.96-4.84T) and Method 2 (domestic implied ~RMB 5.2T from ECDB).

verification.verifier_notes: This is a fourth independent method that reinforces the domestic GMV range. Key uncertainties: parcel share (could be 30-45%) and AOV (could be 40-65). The range deliberately reflects this uncertainty.
